proposals.title: Multi-Parent Recombination
proposals.rationale: The current replacement model is parent-to-child mutation. Deterministic crossover would combine successful traits while preserving a verifiable ancestry DAG.
acceptance: Two distinct active parents can produce a candidate using a versioned crossover operator.
Identical revision, parents, operator, and seed produce a byte-identical child.
Each receipt records ordered parent digests and a field-level inheritance and mutation map.
Ledger verification rejects missing parents, self-parenting, and ancestry cycles.
Single-parent evolution remains supported without changing existing receipt semantics.

proposals.title: Adaptive Mutation Operators
proposals.rationale: A deterministic operator portfolio with receipt-derived credit assignment can sustain improvement when any single mutation strategy stops producing accepted descendants.
acceptance: Every proposal records a stable operator ID, version, parameters, and seed.
Operator selection is deterministic for the same ledger revision and policy.
Invalid or uncommitted proposals receive no success credit.
A configurable exploration floor prevents permanent operator starvation.
Synthetic histories demonstrate selection shifting toward operators with higher accepted improvement.

proposals.title: Stagnation Detection and Recovery
proposals.rationale: Explicit plateau detection can distinguish genuine frontier exhaustion from transient failures and trigger bounded exploration without weakening admission standards.
acceptance: Stagnation is detected from configurable improvement epsilon and receipt window values scoped to a fitness epoch.
The same ledger revision and policy always produce the same stagnation state.
Recovery actions remain within autonomy budgets and never lower the required acceptance margin.
Recovery actions and their triggering evidence are recorded in evolution receipts.
Exhausted recovery budgets terminate with an explicit plateau result rather than retrying indefinitely.

proposals.title: Lineage-Aware Selection
proposals.rationale: Phenotype diversity does not prevent one prolific family from occupying the frontier. Ancestry-aware selection can preserve founder diversity and reduce genealogical collapse.
acceptance: Deterministic relatedness and effective-founder metrics are derived from the lineage DAG.
Configured kinship limits prevent closely related mating when an eligible alternative exists.
Lineage occupancy constraints interoperate with quality-diversity niche admission rules.
Selection policy changes do not rewrite fitness scores or historical receipts.
The observatory reports frontier ancestry concentration and founder survival.

proposals.title: Fitness Epoch Calibration
proposals.rationale: Versioning preserves historical scores, but calibrated benchmark panels are needed to determine whether frontier progress remains real when fitness definitions change.
acceptance: A content-addressed benchmark panel spans multiple lineages, niches, and score ranges.
Each fitness epoch produces a deterministic calibration artifact against that panel.
Activation of a new epoch fails when benchmark coverage or declared calibration checks are unmet.
New receipts retain raw scores while referencing the calibration digest used for cross-epoch comparison.
Historical receipts remain immutable and ledger verification detects missing or altered calibration artifacts.

proposals.title: Deterministic Consensus Runoff
proposals.rationale: Recorded council cycles repeatedly end without consensus. A bounded second-round ballot over an immutable first-round shortlist can improve convergence without adding council members or unbounded deliberation.
acceptance: The first round accepts responses from exactly eight distinct strategy IDs before creating a ballot.
The runoff ballot is digest-addressed, deterministically ordered, and contains only eligible first-round feature IDs.
Each strategy receives the identical immutable ballot and may vote only for listed feature IDs.
Runoff calls are policy-capped, and ties or unmet thresholds produce an explicit no-consensus result.
Receipts preserve both rounds and reproduce the same decision digest during replay.

proposals.title: Durable Council Run Resumption
proposals.rationale: Local CLI and brainstem processes can terminate between strategy calls. A persisted state machine can resume safely without repeating completed calls or producing duplicate decisions.
acceptance: Restarting after three completed strategy responses requests only the remaining five responses.
Concurrent attempts for the same snapshot digest cannot acquire overlapping active leases.
Resumption fails closed when the repository snapshot, policy digest, or strategy roster differs.
An interrupted and uninterrupted run produce the same final decision digest from identical responses.
Completion atomically marks the run final so another invocation performs no provider calls.

proposals.title: Digest-Bound Human Approval Gates
proposals.rationale: Policy-denied side effects currently require broad configuration changes. Narrow, one-time approvals would let operators authorize a specific publish or promotion while preserving bounded autonomy.
acceptance: A denied side effect creates a content-addressed approval request and performs no external mutation.
An approval is bound to the exact action, input digest, policy revision, expiration, and budget.
Expired, altered, replayed, or already-consumed approvals fail closed.
Approval consumption and the authorized side effect are recorded atomically in the operation receipt.
Actions outside the approved scope remain denied.

proposals.title: Brainstem and Copilot CLI Capability Preflight
proposals.rationale: Provider failures should be discovered before mutation or budget consumption. A shared preflight can verify local availability, authentication, protocol compatibility, and enforced tool restrictions.
acceptance: Missing executables, failed authentication, and unsupported output protocols return distinct structured errors before warehouse mutation.
The preflight verifies that no-tools operation and response-size limits can be enforced.
Provider selection follows explicit policy and never silently falls back to another provider.
Successful receipts record provider identity, executable version, capability digest, and preflight result.
Fixture tests cover available, missing, unauthenticated, and incompatible providers.

proposals.title: Council Feature Roadmap Ledger
proposals.rationale: Council orchestration needs durable memory of proposed, accepted, rejected, and completed work so future cycles exclude completed features and distinguish reconsideration from accidental duplication.
acceptance: The ledger assigns each canonical feature ID exactly one current lifecycle state while retaining append-only transitions.
Every strategy prompt includes a sorted immutable completed-feature set and its digest.
Proposals using a completed canonical feature ID are excluded before voting and recorded with a duplicate reason.
State transitions reject unknown predecessors, stale revisions, and conflicting concurrent updates.
Replaying the ledger reconstructs the same eligible proposal set and roadmap digest.

proposals.title: Trusted submission ingestion
proposals.rationale: The public issue-to-gist path crosses untrusted identity and network boundaries before repository mutation. Validation must also establish approval, immutable source revision, and exact-byte provenance.
acceptance: Untrusted issues cannot mutate cards, indexes, or warehouse data until a repository member approval event is independently verified.
Ingestion accepts only GitHub Gist identifiers through the GitHub API, pins a concrete revision, rejects redirects and arbitrary URLs, and enforces size and runtime limits before parsing.
Every accepted submission records the issue ID, submitting actor, approving actor, gist owner, gist revision, and SHA-256 of the fetched bytes.
Tests reject forged approval, actor mismatch, changed gist revisions, oversized responses, redirects, and non-GitHub URLs.

proposals.title: GitHub Actions trust-boundary hardening
proposals.rationale: The workflows can generate artifacts and mutate repository state, making action dependencies, token permissions, event context, and shell interpolation privileged trust boundaries.
acceptance: Every external action reference is pinned to a full commit SHA, and an automated check rejects mutable tags or branches.
Every job declares minimal explicit permissions; write permissions exist only on jobs that require them.
Fork pull requests and other untrusted events receive neither write-capable tokens nor repository secrets.
Workflow tests reject direct interpolation of untrusted event fields into shell commands and reject write jobs lacking trusted event and ref guards.

proposals.title: Fail-closed sandbox egress enforcement
proposals.rationale: The documented two-hatch sandbox boundary should be technically enforced so autonomous code, ingestion, promotion, and provider processes cannot create undeclared network paths or exfiltrate repository data.
acceptance: Outbound access is denied by default and requires a named policy capability with exact HTTPS scheme, host, and port constraints.
Redirect destinations and resolved addresses are revalidated, with loopback, private, link-local, and metadata endpoints rejected.
Provider subprocesses receive a sanitized environment and only policy-authorized egress; execution fails closed when required isolation is unavailable.
Integration tests attempt arbitrary DNS, redirects, private-address access, and denied provider egress and confirm no mutation or receipt is produced.

proposals.title: Signing key rotation and revocation
proposals.rationale: Signed lineage operations require an explicit key lifecycle and rollback-resistant trust root so compromised, expired, or incorrectly scoped keys cannot continue authorizing mutations.
acceptance: A signed, versioned trust policy defines each key ID, algorithm, operation scopes, validity window, and revocation time.
Unknown, expired, revoked, or scope-mismatched keys are rejected before any mutation occurs.
Historical signatures remain verifiable only when an anchored ledger timestamp predates revocation; signatures after revocation are rejected.
Tests cover overlapping rotation, emergency revocation, trust-policy rollback, unknown keys, and operation-scope escalation.

proposals.title: Hologram iframe and browser isolation
proposals.rationale: Remote hologram execution and repository-sourced card data cross a browser trust boundary. Compromised content must not navigate the parent, access same-origin state, open windows, or inject markup.
acceptance: All hologram frames are created through one origin-validating helper with sandbox and no-referrer policies.
Frame sandboxing excludes same-origin access, top navigation, popups, and forms while retaining only capabilities proven necessary for rendering.
Content Security Policy restricts frame origins and disables objects, plugins, and base-URI replacement.
Browser tests confirm valid holograms render while malicious card fields, navigation attempts, popups, and storage access are blocked.

proposals.title: Seeded Evolution Campaign
proposals.rationale: Turn the existing drafts, frontier, fitness epochs, and triple jump into a finite game loop with resource decisions, branching objectives, escalating rivals, and clear victory or defeat conditions.
acceptance: Each run pins its seed, frontier revision, fitness epoch, and campaign definition.
Identical starting state and player choices produce an identical final receipt.
Players manage finite mutation currency and rerolls across at least two branching routes.
Campaign actions do not modify the warehouse unless the player explicitly promotes a winning lineage.
Completed, failed, exported, and resumed runs preserve the same deterministic outcome.

proposals.title: Deterministic Creature Battles
proposals.rationale: Add strategic competition beyond scalar strength by deriving battle attributes from each Moment and letting players choose constrained actions whose timing and matchup determine the winner.
acceptance: Battle attributes are deterministically derived from validated Moment data under a versioned ruleset.
Each turn requires a legal player action and rejects actions exceeding its energy budget.
Identical competitors, seed, and action sequence produce the same turn log and winner in Python and the browser.
Battle receipts include competitor digests, ruleset version, seed, actions, and outcome.
Automated matchup tests demonstrate that no single available action defeats every other action.

proposals.title: Daily Seeded Challenges
proposals.rationale: A shared daily objective gives players a repeatable reason to return while making scores comparable because everyone receives the same organism, constraints, and mutation budget.
acceptance: The same UTC date and pinned frontier revision generate a byte-identical challenge.
Every entrant receives the same parent, objective, budget, fitness epoch, and allowed actions.
The challenge page shows time remaining, attempt history, and the player's deterministic best score.
A share code reconstructs and verifies the complete run without trusting its claimed score.
Date rollover tests prove that published challenges remain immutable after the next challenge begins.

proposals.title: Verified Ranked Leaderboards
proposals.rationale: Convert replay-verifiable challenge and battle receipts into meaningful asynchronous competition with tamper-resistant standings, deterministic ties, and archived seasons.
acceptance: Submitted scores are recomputed from a valid challenge or battle receipt before ranking.
Receipts with altered inputs, unknown rulesets, stale challenge IDs, or mismatched scores are rejected.
Standings use a documented deterministic tie-break rule.
Season closure creates an immutable archive while a new season starts with empty standings.
The leaderboard exposes ruleset, season, challenge, and replay links for every ranked entry.

proposals.title: Lineage Achievement Progression
proposals.rationale: Give long-term purpose to evolving, collecting, battling, and replaying by deriving mastery milestones from immutable activity rather than relying on forgeable client-side counters.
acceptance: Achievement state is recomputed solely from verified lineage, challenge, and battle receipts.
Unlock evaluation is idempotent and cannot award the same achievement twice.
The Holodex displays locked, in-progress, and completed milestones with numeric progress.
Definitions include milestones for collection breadth, campaign completion, battle mastery, and phenotype diversity.
Exported progression profiles are content-addressed and reject tampered receipt references.

proposals.title: Accessible responsive navigation and page shell
proposals.rationale: The growing set of gallery, arena, lab, observatory, and Holodex pages needs consistent navigation, focus behavior, and mobile layout so users can move between experiences without relearning controls.
acceptance: Every primary page exposes consistent skip navigation, landmarks, heading order, and a visible keyboard focus indicator.
At 320 CSS pixels, primary content and navigation have no horizontal overflow.
All navigation and controls are operable by keyboard and have touch targets at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels.
Automated accessibility scans report no critical or serious WCAG 2.1 AA violations.

proposals.title: Adaptive hologram rendering and reduced-motion controls
proposals.rationale: Many live hologram iframes and an animated 3D arena can overwhelm mobile GPUs, batteries, and motion-sensitive users; rendering should follow visibility, device capacity, and explicit user preference.
acceptance: Offscreen holograms stop rendering and resume only after becoming visible.
The number of simultaneously active hologram renderers is capped and verified on a populated gallery.
The experience honors prefers-reduced-motion and provides a persistent in-page motion toggle.
When WebGL is unavailable or context creation fails, each affected surface presents usable static content instead of a blank region.

proposals.title: Semantic list alternative for the 3D arena
proposals.rationale: A 3D-only ranking view excludes screen-reader, keyboard, low-vision, and WebGL-constrained users. A synchronized semantic view preserves the arena's core comparison and evolution workflow.
acceptance: The list view exposes every active organism in the same weakest-to-strongest order as the 3D arena.
Each entry includes an accessible name, strength, rank, and evolution eligibility.
Keyboard selection in either view synchronizes the selected organism and moves focus predictably.
The list and evolution action remain usable when WebGL is disabled.

proposals.title: Holodex search, sorting, and shareable state
proposals.rationale: As the card catalog and federation grow, users need fast discovery and links that preserve what they were viewing, especially on constrained mobile screens.
acceptance: Users can search cards by displayed text and sort by title or strength without a page reload.
The current query, sort, and selected card are represented in the URL and restored after reload or link sharing.
Result counts and empty states are announced to assistive technology without moving focus unexpectedly.
Filtering 1,000 fixture cards completes within 100 milliseconds on the test runner and does not create horizontal overflow at 320 CSS pixels.

proposals.title: Frontend diagnostics and recoverable failure states
proposals.rationale: Static clients currently depend on JSON, iframes, and WebGL without an evident shared diagnostic surface. Structured local telemetry and actionable errors would make broken data, embeds, and performance regressions detectable.
acceptance: Forced JSON fetch failures, iframe timeouts, and WebGL initialization failures each display an actionable error with retry behavior.
PerformanceObserver captures LCP, INP, and CLS alongside page-ready and hologram-failure events in a bounded local buffer.
A diagnostic view exports event names, timestamps, page identifiers, and correlation IDs without card payloads, tokens, or user-identifying data.
No diagnostic data leaves the browser unless an explicit opt-in transport is configured.

proposals.title: Adversarial Fitness Regression Suite
proposals.rationale: Continuously searches for reward-hacking inputs so improvements to the scorer cannot silently reopen Goodhart exploits already addressed by current fitness epochs.
acceptance: A fixed-seed mutation search covers frame duplication, temporal jitter, clipping, path padding, and stress-masking operators.
Every supported fitness version declares testable invariance and degradation expectations for the adversarial corpus.
CI fails with a minimized reproducible counterexample when a degrading mutation improves fitness contrary to its declared expectation.
Two runs from the same corpus, seed, and fitness version produce byte-identical audit results.

proposals.title: Perturbation-Based Fitness Confidence Bounds
proposals.rationale: Selection based on a deterministic lower confidence bound rewards candidates whose quality survives small input perturbations instead of brittle point-score exploits.
acceptance: Candidate digests deterministically generate a bounded panel of temporal, positional, and numeric perturbations.
Acceptance uses the configured lower quantile rather than the unperturbed point score.
A fixture that clears the point-score margin but fails the confidence bound is rejected without changing the frontier.
Evolution receipts record the perturbation policy digest, sample count, minimum, median, lower quantile, and acceptance bound.

proposals.title: Pareto Fitness Selection
proposals.rationale: A versioned epsilon-Pareto policy prevents one highly optimized component from compensating for severe regressions in vitality, articulation, motion, smoothness, or radiance.
acceptance: The scorer exposes a stable named objective vector alongside its aggregate score.
Dominated candidates are rejected and epsilon-nondominated candidates use deterministic digest-based tie-breaking within a fixed archive cap.
A candidate with a higher aggregate score but a policy-breaking objective regression is rejected in a regression test.
Receipts and the Evolution Observatory show objective vectors, dominance comparisons, policy version, and the decisive selection reason.

proposals.title: Fitness Attribution Reports
proposals.rationale: Exact per-term attribution and counterfactual deltas make score changes auditable, reveal accidental proxy optimization, and explain why a candidate passed or failed.
acceptance: Each report contains raw measurements, normalization, weights, gates, penalties, contributions, and final score for the selected epoch.
Recombining reported contributions reproduces the canonical score within the declared numeric tolerance.
Deterministic counterfactuals quantify the score delta from neutralizing each objective while holding all others fixed.
CLI audit output and the Evolution Observatory render identical attribution values for current and historical receipts.

proposals.title: Lineage Diversity Guardrails
proposals.rationale: Phenotype niches alone can hide ancestry monocultures; lineage concentration limits preserve independent evolutionary paths and make diversity claims harder to game.
acceptance: The active frontier reports effective lineage count, maximum ancestor share, and ancestry entropy from verified lineage records.
A versioned policy rejects an admission that breaches lineage-concentration limits even when it occupies a distinct phenotype niche.
A fixture with distinct descriptors but one shared ancestor triggers the guardrail, while an equally sized multi-ancestor frontier passes.
Every decision receipt records before-and-after lineage metrics and the exact guardrail outcome.

proposals.title: Exactly-once scheduled evolution runs
proposals.rationale: Workflow overlap, reruns, and push races can invoke providers twice or produce divergent mutations despite atomic file transactions. Stable operation IDs, non-cancelling concurrency, and durable terminal outcomes make each scheduled trigger execute once.
acceptance: Concurrent runs for the same operation ID produce one provider invocation and at most one evolution receipt.
Rerunning a completed operation returns its recorded result without changing repository artifacts.
A process terminated at each persistence boundary recovers to either the unchanged prior state or one valid terminal receipt.

proposals.title: Deterministic warehouse disaster recovery
proposals.rationale: The repository has several derived catalogs and observatories, but no demonstrated recovery path when they are deleted or corrupted. A single verified rebuild procedure turns immutable history into an operational backup.
acceptance: Recovery from authoritative observations and receipts reproduces frontier, lineages, cards, indexes, and manifests byte-for-byte.
Recovery writes through a staging directory and leaves existing artifacts unchanged when verification fails.
Missing content, broken hashes, and dangling lineage references each cause a nonzero exit with an actionable diagnostic.

proposals.title: Continuous repository integrity audits
proposals.rationale: Hash chains, fitness epochs, manifests, and generated views can drift independently between scheduled mutations. A read-only recurring audit detects corruption before another evolution builds upon it.
acceptance: A scheduled and manually dispatchable workflow verifies ledger chains, lineage references, stored fitness, frontier membership, and generated-artifact freshness.
The audit emits a machine-readable report containing the checked revision, tool versions, checks, durations, and final status.
Tampering with any receipt, score, lineage reference, or generated artifact makes the workflow fail without modifying repository data.

proposals.title: Deterministic fault-injection test matrix
proposals.rationale: Transaction and retry guarantees need systematic proof under interruption, malformed provider responses, concurrent writers, and filesystem failures rather than only successful-path tests.
acceptance: Deterministic failpoints exercise every evolution, warehouse, and ingest persistence boundary in CI.
Each injected failure leaves no partial receipt, stale lock, temporary file, duplicate observation, or mismatched generated artifact.
Tests cover malformed and oversized provider output, retry exhaustion, concurrent writers, and recovery on the next invocation.

proposals.title: Operational telemetry and stale-run alerts
proposals.rationale: Workflow success alone does not reveal repeated no-ops, exhausted budgets, retry storms, latency regressions, or missed schedules. Structured summaries and a heartbeat provide actionable operational visibility without storing model content.
acceptance: Every harness and ingest exit path emits schema-validated telemetry with operation ID, revision, duration, attempts, budget use, outcome, and no-op or failure reason.
Telemetry excludes prompts, responses, credentials, and environment secrets, with redaction tests covering representative inputs.
A watchdog reports unhealthy status when no successful scheduled run occurs within twice its cadence and automatically clears after a successful run.

proposals.title: One-command first-jump demo
proposals.rationale: Convert the dense quickstart into a safe, deterministic product experience that demonstrates the full loop without credentials, network access, or mutations to the canonical warehouse.
acceptance: Running `python3 tools/demo.py --output <empty-directory>` completes using only repository dependencies and no network or provider authentication.
The demo performs one deterministic accepted jump and produces viewable frontier, card, and lineage artifacts in the output directory.
The command leaves every tracked repository file byte-for-byte unchanged.
Failures identify the unmet prerequisite and an actionable remediation command.
README.md takes a new user from clone to viewing the demo result in no more than three commands.

proposals.title: Verifiable read-only federation sync
proposals.rationale: Turn the existing peer listing into differentiated, trustworthy cross-repository discovery while deliberately excluding remote writes and their operational burden.
acceptance: The sync command validates each peer manifest and referenced catalog digest before including remote entries.
Merged entries preserve peer origin, source URL, content digest, and last successful observation time.
Identical local and peer inputs produce byte-identical federation artifacts regardless of fetch order.
Malformed, unreachable, or stale peers are visibly quarantined without removing valid local catalog entries.
The Holodex can filter by peer and distinguishes local, verified remote, stale, and quarantined entries.
Federation remains read-only and cannot modify local frontier, lineage, or evolution ledgers.

proposals.title: Portable lineage passports
proposals.rationale: Give every organism one comprehensible identity surface connecting its card, fitness, ancestry, current status, and replay receipt across the otherwise fragmented experiences.
acceptance: Artifact generation emits one passport for every cataloged organism using only validated warehouse sources.
Each passport includes the organism digest, origin, active or retired status, parent digest, fitness epoch, score, and receipt reference.
Gallery, Arena, Holodex, and Observatory links resolve to the same passport identity without duplicating lineage logic in each page.
A passport whose referenced digest or receipt is missing fails artifact validation rather than displaying partial provenance.
Repeated builds from unchanged inputs produce byte-identical passports.

proposals.title: Unified product navigation shell
proposals.rationale: Make Gallery, Arena, Evolution Lab, Observatory, and Holodex feel like one product with a clear journey rather than independent demonstrations.
acceptance: Every product page renders the same ordered navigation, terminology, and active-page indicator from shared assets.
Navigation works with keyboard controls and at mobile viewport widths without obscuring page controls.
Existing query parameters, hash deep links, and direct page URLs continue to work.
Each page exposes a contextual next action that links to the relevant existing workflow.
A single shell implementation supplies navigation behavior; pages do not contain divergent copied menus.

proposals.title: Goal-oriented evolution presets
proposals.rationale: Present the existing fitness epochs and selection policies as understandable user goals, reducing configuration complexity without expanding the scoring or selection surface.
acceptance: A versioned manifest defines named presets and resolves each exclusively to existing fitness-version and selection-policy options.
The CLI accepts a preset and produces the same result as its resolved explicit flags for an identical seed and input revision.
The Evolution Lab displays each preset's goal and tradeoff before candidate generation.
Accepted receipts record both the preset identifier and resolved options so replay does not depend on mutable labels.
Unknown, incompatible, or changed preset definitions fail validation with no warehouse mutation.
